title: valeriana repens var. calvescens
intro: Valeriana repens var. calvescens, also known as creeping valerian, is a perennial herb that belongs to the valerian family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its sedative, antispasmodic, and antianxiety effects. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to alleviate symptoms of insomnia, restlessness, and other sleep disorders.
